articles.summary: If QA is a bottleneck on your software engineering team and you're releasing slowly because of it, you must check out QA Wolf.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
MUSK SAYS XAI MUST BE REBUILT AS CO-FOUNDERS EXIT
NVIDIA'S GTC WILL MARK AN AI CHIP PIVOT. HERE'S WHY THE CPU IS TAKING CENTER STAGE
articles.summary: Nvidia plans to unveil details about its agentic optimized CPUs at its annual GTC conference kicking off today. CPUs are becoming a bottleneck in the AI and agentic workflow. Agentic AI requires a lot of general compute power as they move large amounts of data around for AI workflows. The CPU market growth rate could exceed GPU growth by 2028. Leading CPU providers have warned customers in China of supply shortages, and lead times and prices have already increased.

articles.title: TRAVIS KALANICK DEBUTS PLAN FOR ‘GAINFULLY EMPLOYED ROBOTS'
articles.summary: Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick has launched a venture focused on creating gainfully employed robots for the food, mining, and transport industries. Atoms has thousands of employees and has been in stealth for eight years. Its plans include infrastructure for better food, more productive mines, and a wheelbase for robots in transportation.

articles.title: CHINA APPROVES FIRST BRAIN IMPLANT FOR COMMERCIAL USE
articles.summary: China has approved its first-ever invasive brain-computer interface (BCI) product, a device for adult patients who are partially paralyzed due to spinal cord injury. In trials, the device helped patients improve their ability to hold and grab objects with their hands. BCI companies receive backing from the state as the technology is one of the six strategic industries of the future in its latest five year plan. The Chinese government has pledged to streamline regulatory review and set up reimbursement guidelines.

articles.title: COMPREHENSION DEBT — THE HIDDEN COST OF AI GENERATED CODE
articles.summary: Comprehension debt is a hidden cost to human intelligence and memory that results from excessive reliance on AI and automation. It is the growing gap between how much code exists in a system and how much of it any human being genuinely understands. It can lead to teams not being able to explain why design decisions had been made or how different parts of the system are supposed to work together. The debt has to be paid sooner or later, and interest accrues rapidly.

articles.title: THE "NIGHT SHIFT" AGENTIC WORKFLOW
articles.summary: This post outlines an agentic workflow that allows developers and agents to work together around the clock. The developer takes the 'day shift', while AI agents work overnight. The developer prepares as much as they can during the day shift for the agents to work autonomously as they rest, finishing tasks by the next morning. The workflow has helped the author spend less time babysitting and much more time thinking about the problems they need to solve.

articles.title: META PLANNING SWEEPING LAYOFFS AS AI COSTS MOUNT
NEO SYMBOLIC CAPITALISM
articles.summary: Symbolic capital is reputation - the accumulated weight of being someone worth listening to. It helps things convert better, faster. There is a lot of symbolic capitalism to be earned these days thanks to social media. The people with the most power and the most to gain have turned X into a gaming platform for symbolic capital.

articles.title: BIG IRON BITS: THE MAINFRAME MOCKUMENTARY YOU NEVER KNEW YOU NEEDED
articles.summary: The mainframe is thriving! Big Iron Bits: a Mainframe Mockumentary explains why everyone needs Big Iron. Think _The I.T. Crowd_ meets _Silicon Valley_.

articles.title: HOW KARPATHY'S AUTORESEARCH WORKS AND WHAT YOU CAN LEARN FROM IT
articles.summary: Autoresearch shows how autonomous systems become much more useful when you reduce them to a tight harness with clear boundaries, a stable metric, reversible experiments, and good operational discipline.

articles.title: STAFF COMPLAIN THAT XAI IS FLAILING BECAUSE OF CONSTANT UPHEAVAL
articles.summary: Elon Musk has ordered another round of job cuts at xAI, saying that the company was not built right the first time so it is being rebuilt from the foundations up.

articles.title: MUSK'S LATEST MOONSHOT MULTI-BILLION PROJECT LAUNCHES ON A SATURDAY
articles.summary: Musk didn't elaborate on any details about the project, but his previous statements indicate that it is a semiconductor facility project that will enable his companies to get enough supply of AI accelerators.

articles.title: DEAR ALIENS - A WRITING COMPETITION FOR HUMANS
articles.summary: Dear Aliens is an open-ended competition to write humanity's first message to aliens. Run by the folks behind funnest.world, free to enter and $2k cash prize for the winner!

articles.title: GOOGLE FIBER WILL BE SOLD TO PRIVATE EQUITY FIRM AND MERGE WITH CABLE COMPANY
articles.summary: GFiber is being sold to private equity firm Stonepeak and will be combined with Astound Broadband to create a larger Internet service provider.

articles.title: PORTING SOFTWARE HAS BEEN TRIVIAL FOR A WHILE NOW. HERE'S HOW YOU DO IT
articles.summary: How to port any codebase from one language to another using Ralph loops.

articles.title: THE DEATH OF “GOOD ENOUGH”
articles.summary: 'Good enough' was previously restricted by time, but AI makes things a lot faster.
